mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, discount rates and recession odds decline together, lifting equity valuations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below its value 3 months earlier, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and profit expectations, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates stronger consumer spending and payroll income, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO relative to its recent level signals improving industrial output and earnings momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not higher when INDPRO is above its 3-month moving average, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains anchored below the inflation trend, real policy rates fall and equities should reprice upward over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises faster than FEDFUNDS over the prior 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if episodes of inflation running above policy-rate growth do not generate positive 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the conditional return spread is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after a prior peak signals a cooling housing cycle and weaker cyclically sensitive demand, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 5% from its 3-month high
failure_condition: Reject if falling HOUST is not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average return difference versus non-triggered periods is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A payroll contraction in PAYEMS combined with a simultaneous rise in UNRATE identifies a genuine labor-market deterioration, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS down / UNRATE up condition does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the interaction adds no predictive value over either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a reopening phase, rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS signals broad capacity utilization and earnings support, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in INDPRO and PAYEMS fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns better than either proxy alone, or if the hit rate falls below 55% in rolling out-of-sample tests.
